verses.original:
पुर नर भरत प्रीति मैं गाई। मति अनुरूप अनूप सुहाई।।
अब प्रभु चरित सुनहु अति पावन। करत जे बन सुर नर मुनि भावन।।
verses.transliteration:
pura nara bharata prīti maiṃ gāī. mati anurūpa anūpa suhāī.
aba prabhu carita sunahu ati pāvana. karata je bana sura nara muni bhāvana.
verses.translation: I have sung of the love of the people of the city and of Bharat, as best as my intellect could portray that incomparable beauty. Now hear the most sacred deeds that the Lord performed in the forest, which delight gods, men, and sages.

verses.original:
एक बार चुनि कुसुम सुहाए। निज कर भूषन राम बनाए।।
सीतहि पहिराए प्रभु सादर। बैठे फटिक सिला पर सुंदर।।
verses.transliteration:
eka bāra cuni kusuma suhāe. nija kara bhūṣana rāma banāe.
sītahi pahirāe prabhu sādara. baiṭhe phaṭika silā para sundara.
verses.translation: Once, having gathered beautiful flowers, Rama made ornaments with His own hands. He lovingly adorned Sita with them, and then the two sat together on a beautiful crystal rock.

verses.original:
सुरपति सुत धरि बायस बेषा। सठ चाहत रघुपति बल देखा।।
जिमि पिपीलिका सागर थाहा। महा मंदमति पावन चाहा।।
verses.transliteration:
surapati suta dhari bāyasa beṣā. saṭha cāhata raghupati bala dekhā.
jimi pipīlikā sāgara thāhā. mahā mandamati pāvana cāhā.
verses.translation: Indra's son, disguising himself as a crow, foolishly wished to test the power of Rama. Just as an ant might try to fathom the depth of the ocean, so this greatly foolish one desired the impossible.

verses.original:
सीता चरन चौंच हति भागा। मूढ़ मंदमति कारन कागा।।
चला रुधिर रघुनायक जाना। सींक धनुष सायक संधाना।।
verses.transliteration:
sītā carana cauṃca hati bhāgā. mūḍha mandamati kārana kāgā.
calā rudhira raghunāyaka jānā. sīṃka dhanuṣa sāyaka sandhānā.
verses.translation: He struck Sita's foot with his beak and fled, the fool, the dim-witted wretch in crow form. Blood began to flow; the Lord of the Raghus noticed and fitted an arrow of sacred grass to His bow.

verses.original:
अति कृपाल रघुनायक सदा दीन पर नेह।
ता सन आइ कीन्ह छलु मूरख अवगुन गेह।।1।।
verses.transliteration:
ati kṛpāla raghunāyaka sadā dīna para neha.
tā sana āi kīnha chalu mūrakha avaguna geha. 1.
verses.translation: The most gracious Lord of the Raghus always has affection for the humble. Yet against Him this fool, the abode of faults, came and committed treachery. ||1||

verses.original:
प्रेरित मंत्र ब्रह्मसर धावा। चला भाजि बायस भय पावा।।
धरि निज रुप गयउ पितु पाहीं। राम बिमुख राखा तेहि नाहीं।।
verses.transliteration:
prerita mantra brahmasara dhāvā. calā bhāji bāyasa bhaya pāvā.
dhari nija rūpa gayau pitu pāhīṃ. rāma bimukha rākhā tehi nāhīṃ.
verses.translation: Propelled by the sacred mantra, the Brahma-arrow pursued him. The crow fled in terror. Assuming his own form, he went to his father (Indra), but none would shelter one who opposed Rama.

verses.original:
भा निरास उपजी मन त्रासा। जथा चक्र भय रिषि दुर्बासा।।
ब्रह्मधाम सिवपुर सब लोका। फिरा श्रमित ब्याकुल भय सोका।।
verses.transliteration:
bhā nirāsa upajī mana trāsā. jathā cakra bhaya riṣi durbāsā.
brahmadhāma sivapura saba lokā. phirā śramita byākula bhaya sokā.
verses.translation: He became hopeless and terror arose in his mind, like the sage Durvasa's fear of Vishnu's discus. He wandered through Brahma's realm, Shiva's city, and all the worlds, exhausted, distressed with fear and sorrow.

verses.original:
काहूँ बैठन कहा न ओही। राखि को सकइ राम कर द्रोही।।
मातु मृत्यु पितु समन समाना। सुधा होइ बिष सुनु हरिजाना।।
verses.transliteration:
kāhūṃ baiṭhana kahā na ohī. rākhi ko sakai rāma kara drohī.
mātu mṛtyu pitu samana samānā. sudhā hoi biṣa sunu harijānā.
verses.translation: No one told him to sit down anywhere. Who can protect one who is hostile to Rama? Mother becomes death, father becomes Yama — hear, O devotee of Hari — even nectar becomes poison.

verses.original:
मित्र करइ सत रिपु कै करनी। ता कहँ बिबुधनदी बैतरनी।।
सब जगु ताहि अनलहु ते ताता। जो रघुबीर बिमुख सुनु भ्राता।।
verses.transliteration:
mitra karai sata ripu kai karanī. tā kahaṃ bibudhanadī baitaranī.
saba jagu tāhi analahu te tātā. jo raghubīra bimukha sunu bhrātā.
verses.translation: A friend acts like a hundred enemies toward him; the celestial Ganga becomes the river Vaitarni (river of hell) for him. The whole world is like fire to him — listen, brother — who turns away from Raghubir.

verses.original:
नारद देखा बिकल जयंता। लागि दया कोमल चित संता।।
पठवा तुरत राम पहिं ताही। कहेसि पुकारि प्रनत हित पाही।।
verses.transliteration:
nārada dekhā bikala jayantā. lāgi dayā komala cita santā.
paṭhavā turata rāma pahiṃ tāhī. kahesi pukāri pranata hita pāhī.
verses.translation: Narada saw the distressed Jayanta. The saint, tender of heart, felt compassion. He immediately sent him to Rama, telling him: 'Go and cry out — the Lord protects those who seek refuge.'

verses.original:
आतुर सभय गहेसि पद जाई। त्राहि त्राहि दयाल रघुराई।।
अतुलित बल अतुलित प्रभुताई। मैं मतिमंद जानि नहिं पाई।।
verses.transliteration:
ātura sabhaya gahesi pada jāī. trāhi trāhi dayāla raghurāī.
atulita bala atulita prabhutāī. maiṃ matimanda jāni nahiṃ pāī.
verses.translation: Anxious and afraid, he went and clasped the Lord's feet, crying: 'Save me, save me, O merciful Lord of the Raghus! Your strength and might are immeasurable; I, being dull-witted, failed to recognize them.'

verses.original:
निज कृत कर्म जनित फल पायउँ। अब प्रभु पाहि सरन तकि आयउँ।।
सुनि कृपाल अति आरत बानी। एकनयन करि तजा भवानी।।
verses.transliteration:
nija kṛta karma janita phala pāyauṃ. aba prabhu pāhi sarana taki āyauṃ.
suni kṛpāla ati ārata bānī. ekanyana kari tajā bhavānī.
verses.translation: I have received the fruit born of my own actions. Now, Lord, protect me — I have come seeking Your refuge. Hearing his most pitiful words, the Compassionate One made him one-eyed and released him, O Bhavani.

verses.original:
कीन्ह मोह बस द्रोह जद्यपि तेहि कर बध उचित।
प्रभु छाड़ेउ करि छोह को कृपाल रघुबीर सम।।2।।
verses.transliteration:
kīnha moha basa droha jadyapi tehi kara badha ucita.
prabhu chāḍeu kari choha ko kṛpāla raghubīra sama. 2.
verses.translation: Though he committed treachery under the spell of delusion and deserved to be slain, the Lord released him with compassion. Who is as merciful as Raghubir? ||2||

verses.original:
रघुपति चित्रकूट बसि नाना। चरित किए श्रुति सुधा समाना।।
बहुरि राम अस मन अनुमाना। होइहि भीर सबहिं मोहि जाना।।
verses.transliteration:
raghupati citrakūṭa basi nānā. carita kie śruti sudhā samānā.
bahuri rāma asa mana anumānā. hoihi bhīra sabahiṃ mohi jānā.
verses.translation: After dwelling at Chitrakut and performing many deeds as sweet as the nectar of the Vedas, Rama then thought: 'There will be a great crowd, as everyone now knows Me.'

verses.original:
सकल मुनिन्ह सन बिदा कराई। सीता सहित चले द्वौ भाई।।
अत्रि के आश्रम जब प्रभु गयऊ। सुनत महामुनि हरषित भयऊ।।
verses.transliteration:
sakala muninha sana bidā karāī. sītā sahita cale dvau bhāī.
atri ke āśrama jaba prabhu gayaū. sunata mahāmuni harṣita bhayaū.
verses.translation: Taking leave of all the sages, Rama set out with Sita and His brother. When the Lord reached the hermitage of Atri, the great sage was delighted to hear of His arrival.

verses.original:
पुलकित गात अत्रि उठि धाए। देखि रामु आतुर चलि आए।।
करत दंडवत मुनि उर लाए। प्रेम बारि द्वौ जन अन्हवाए।।
verses.transliteration:
pulakita gāta atri uṭhi dhāe. dekhi rāmu ātura cali āe.
karata daṇḍavata muni ura lāe. prema bāri dvau jana anhavāe.
verses.translation: With hair standing on end, Atri rose and ran. Seeing Rama, he came forward eagerly. As Rama prostrated, the sage embraced Him to his heart and bathed both in tears of love.

verses.original:
देखि राम छबि नयन जुड़ाने। सादर निज आश्रम तब आने।।
करि पूजा कहि बचन सुहाए। दिए मूल फल प्रभु मन भाए।।
verses.transliteration:
dekhi rāma chabi nayana juḍāne. sādara nija āśrama taba āne.
kari pūjā kahi bacana suhāe. die mūla phala prabhu mana bhāe.
verses.translation: His eyes were refreshed by gazing upon Rama's beauty. He then respectfully brought them to his hermitage. Performing worship and speaking sweet words, he offered roots and fruits that pleased the Lord's heart.

verses.original:
प्रभु आसन आसीन भरि लोचन सोभा निरखि।
मुनिबर परम प्रबीन जोरि पानि अस्तुति करत।।3।।
verses.transliteration:
prabhu āsana āsīna bhari locana sobhā nirakhi.
munibara parama prabīna jori pāni astuti karata. 3.
verses.translation: With the Lord seated on His seat, filling his eyes with His beauty, the most wise sage, joining his palms, began to offer praise. ||3||

verses.original:
नमामि भक्त वत्सलं। कृपालु शील कोमलं।।
भजामि ते पदांबुजं। अकामिनां स्वधामदं।।
निकाम श्याम सुंदरं। भवाम्बुनाथ मंदरं।।
प्रफुल्ल कंज लोचनं। मदादि दोष मोचनं।।
प्रलंब बाहु विक्रमं। प्रभोऽप्रमेय वैभवं।।
निषंग चाप सायकं। धरं त्रिलोक नायकं।।
दिनेश वंश मंडनं। महेश चाप खंडनं।।
मुनींद्र संत रंजनं। सुरारि वृंद भंजनं।।
मनोज  वैरि वंदितं। अजादि देव सेवितं।।
विशुद्ध बोध विग्रहं। समस्त दूषणापहं।।
नमामि इंदिरा पतिं। सुखाकरं सतां गतिं।।
भजे सशक्ति सानुजं। शची पतिं प्रियानुजं।।
त्वदंघ्रि मूल ये नराः। भजंति हीन मत्सरा।।
पतंति नो भवार्णवे। वितर्क वीचि संकुले।।
विविक्त वासिनः सदा। भजंति मुक्तये मुदा।।
निरस्य इंद्रियादिकं। प्रयांति ते गतिं स्वकं।।
तमेकमभ्दुतं प्रभुं। निरीहमीश्वरं विभुं।।
जगद्गुरुं च शाश्वतं। तुरीयमेव केवलं।।
भजामि भाव वल्लभं। कुयोगिनां सुदुर्लभं।।
स्वभक्त कल्प पादपं। समं सुसेव्यमन्वहं।।
अनूप रूप भूपतिं। नतोऽहमुर्विजा पतिं।।
प्रसीद मे नमामि ते। पदाब्ज भक्ति देहि मे।।
पठंति ये स्तवं इदं। नरादरेण ते पदं।।
व्रजंति नात्र संशयं। त्वदीय भक्ति संयुता।।
verses.transliteration:
namāmi bhakta vatsalaṃ. kṛpālu śīla komalaṃ.
bhajāmi te padāmbujaṃ. akāmināṃ svadhāmadaṃ.
nikāma śyāma sundaraṃ. bhavāmbunātha mandaraṃ.
praphulla kanja locanaṃ. madādi doṣa mocanaṃ.
pralamba bāhu vikramaṃ. prabhoaprameya vaibhavaṃ.
niṣaṅga cāpa sāyakaṃ. dharaṃ triloka nāyakaṃ.
dineśa vaṃśa maṇḍanaṃ. maheśa cāpa khaṇḍanaṃ.
munīndra santa rañjanaṃ. surāri vṛnda bhañjanaṃ.
manoja vairi vanditaṃ. ajādi deva sevitaṃ.
viśuddha bodha vigrahaṃ. samasta dūṣaṇāpahaṃ.
namāmi indirā patiṃ. sukhākaraṃ satāṃ gatiṃ.
bhaje saśakti sānujaṃ. śacī patiṃ priyānujaṃ.
tvadaṅghri mūla ye narāḥ. bhajanti hīna matsarā.
patanti no bhavārṇave. vitarka vīci saṅkule.
vivikta vāsinaḥ sadā. bhajanti muktaye mudā.
nirasyendri yādikam. prayānti te gatiṃ svakam.
tamekamabhduataṃ prabhuṃ. nirīhamīśvaraṃ vibhuṃ.
jagadguruṃ ca śāśvataṃ. turīyameva kevalaṃ.
bhajāmi bhāva vallabhaṃ. kuyogināṃ sudarlabhaṃ.
svabhakta kalpa pādapaṃ. samaṃ susevyamanvahaṃ.
anūpa rūpa bhūpatiṃ. natoahamurvi jā patiṃ.
prasīda me namāmi te. padābja bhakti dehi me.
paṭhanti ye stavaṃ idaṃ. narādareṇa te padaṃ.
vrajanti nātra saṃśayaṃ. tvadīya bhakti saṃyutā.
verses.translation: I bow to the One who loves His devotees, who is compassionate, virtuous, and gentle. I worship Your lotus feet, which grant the supreme abode to the desireless. Naturally dark and beautiful, You are the Mandara mountain for churning the ocean of worldly existence. With eyes like fully-bloomed lotuses, You destroy faults like pride. With mighty long arms and valor, O Lord of immeasurable glory, You hold the quiver, bow, and arrows, O ruler of the three worlds. Ornament of the Solar dynasty, breaker of Shiva's bow, delighter of great sages and saints, destroyer of hosts of demons. Worshipped by the enemy of Kamadeva (Shiva), served by Brahma and other gods. Embodiment of pure knowledge, remover of all blemishes. I bow to the Lord of Lakshmi, the source of bliss, the refuge of the virtuous. I worship You with Your consort and younger brother, O beloved younger brother of Indra's wife. Those mortals who worship the root of Your feet, free from envy, do not fall into the ocean of worldly existence filled with waves of doubt. Those who dwell in solitude, who worship joyfully for liberation, casting aside the senses and the rest, attain Your own supreme state. That one, wondrous Lord, desireless, the supreme sovereign, all-pervading, the eternal teacher of the universe, the transcendent and absolute alone — I worship the beloved of devotion, most difficult for false yogis to attain, the wish-fulfilling tree for His devotees, equal toward all and worthy of constant service. I bow to the Lord of incomparable form, the king, the Lord of Sita. Be gracious to me — I bow to You — grant me devotion to Your lotus feet. Those who recite this hymn with reverence shall reach Your abode — there is no doubt — endowed with devotion to You.

verses.original:
अनुसुइया के पद गहि सीता। मिली बहोरि सुसील बिनीता।।
रिषिपतिनी मन सुख अधिकाई। आसिष देइ निकट बैठाई।।
verses.transliteration:
anusūyā ke pada gahi sītā. milī bahori susīla binītā.
riṣipatinī mana sukha adhikāī. āsiṣa dei nikaṭa baiṭhāī.
verses.translation: Sita clasped the feet of Anasuya and met her again with great humility and gentle grace. The sage's wife felt great joy in her heart and, blessing Sita, seated her near herself.

verses.original:
दिब्य बसन भूषन पहिराए। जे नित नूतन अमल सुहाए।।
कह रिषिबधू सरस मृदु बानी। नारिधर्म कछु ब्याज बखानी।।
verses.transliteration:
dibya basana bhūṣana pahirāe. je nita nūtana amala suhāe.
kaha riṣibadhū sarasa mṛdu bānī. nāridharma kachu byāja bakhānī.
verses.translation: She dressed Sita in divine garments and ornaments that remained ever new, spotless, and beautiful. Then the sage's wife spoke in sweet, gentle tones, narrating the duties of a wife through stories.

verses.original:
मातु पिता भ्राता हितकारी। मितप्रद सब सुनु राजकुमारी।।
अमित दानि भर्ता बयदेही। अधम सो नारि जो सेव न तेही।।
verses.transliteration:
mātu pitā bhrātā hitakārī. mitaprada saba sunu rājakumārī.
amita dāni bhartā bayadehī. adhama so nāri jo seva na tehī.
verses.translation: Listen, O princess — mother, father, brother, and benefactor all give limited blessings. But the husband is the giver of unlimited gifts, O daughter of Videha. That woman is the lowest who does not serve him.

verses.original:
धीरज धर्म मित्र अरु नारी। आपद काल परिखिअहिं चारी।।
बृद्ध रोगबस जड़ धनहीना। अधं बधिर क्रोधी अति दीना।।
verses.transliteration:
dhīraja dharma mitra aru nārī. āpada kāla parikhiahiṃ cārī.
bṛddha rogabasa jaḍa dhanahīnā. andha badhira krodhī ati dīnā.
verses.translation: Patience, righteousness, a friend, and a wife — these four are tested in times of adversity. Even if the husband is aged, sick, dull, poor, blind, deaf, wrathful, or utterly destitute —

verses.original:
ऐसेहु पति कर किएँ अपमाना। नारि पाव जमपुर दुख नाना।।
एकइ धर्म एक ब्रत नेमा। कायँ बचन मन पति पद प्रेमा।।
verses.transliteration:
aisehu pati kara kieṃ apamānā. nāri pāva jamapura dukha nānā.
ekai dharma eka brata nemā. kāyaṃ bacana mana pati pada premā.
verses.translation: Even such a husband — if a woman disrespects him, she suffers many torments in the abode of Yama (death). There is but one duty, one vow, one discipline: love for the husband's feet in thought, word, and deed.

verses.original:
जग पति ब्रता चारि बिधि अहहिं। बेद पुरान संत सब कहहिं।।
उत्तम के अस बस मन माहीं। सपनेहुँ आन पुरुष जग नाहीं।।
verses.transliteration:
jaga pati bratā cāri bidhi ahahiṃ. beda purāna santa saba kahahiṃ.
uttama ke asa basa mana māhīṃ. sapanehuṃ āna puruṣa jaga nāhīṃ.
verses.translation: There are four types of devoted wives in the world, say the Vedas, Puranas, and saints. The highest is she in whose mind there exists no other man, even in dreams.

verses.original:
मध्यम परपति देखइ कैसें। भ्राता पिता पुत्र निज जैंसें।।
धर्म बिचारि समुझि कुल रहई। सो निकिष्ट त्रिय श्रुति अस कहई।।
verses.transliteration:
madhyama parapati dekhai kaiseṃ. bhrātā pitā putra nija jaiṃseṃ.
dharma bicāri samujhi kula rahaī. so nikiṣṭa triya śruti asa kahaī.
verses.translation: The middling one looks upon other men as she would upon a brother, father, or son. The lowest, say the Vedas, is she who restrains herself only by considering dharma and family honor.

verses.original:
बिनु अवसर भय तें रह जोई। जानेहु अधम नारि जग सोई।।
पति बंचक परपति रति करई। रौरव नरक कल्प सत परई।।
verses.transliteration:
binu avasara bhaya teṃ raha joī. jānehu adhama nāri jaga soī.
pati bañcaka parapati rati karaī. raurava naraka kalpa sata paraī.
verses.translation: She who refrains from sin only out of fear when there is no opportunity — know her to be the lowest of women in the world. She who deceives her husband and delights in another man falls into the Raurava hell for a hundred aeons.

verses.original:
छन सुख लागि जनम सत कोटि। दुख न समुझ तेहि सम को खोटी।।
बिनु श्रम नारि परम गति लहई। पतिब्रत धर्म छाड़ि छल गहई।।
verses.transliteration:
chana sukha lāgi janama sata koṭi. dukha na samujha tehi sama ko khoṭī.
binu śrama nāri parama gati lahaī. pativrata dharma chāḍi chala gahaī.
verses.translation: For a moment's pleasure, she suffers through a hundred crore births — who could be more wretched than such a one? A woman attains the highest state effortlessly by following the vow of devotion to her husband, abandoning all deceit.

verses.original: पति प्रतिकुल जनम जहँ जाई। बिधवा होई पाई तरुनाई।।
verses.transliteration: pati pratikula janama jahaṃ jāī. bidhavā hoi pāī tarunāī.
verses.translation: Wherever she is reborn going against her husband, she becomes a widow in the prime of youth.

verses.original:
सहज अपावनि नारि पति सेवत सुभ गति लहइ।
जसु गावत श्रुति चारि अजहु तुलसिका हरिहि प्रिय।।5(क)।।
सुनु सीता तव नाम सुमिर नारि पतिब्रत करहि।
तोहि प्रानप्रिय राम कहिउँ कथा संसार हित।।5(ख)।।
verses.transliteration:
sahaja apāvani nāri pati sevata subha gati lahai.
jasu gāvata śruti cāri ajahu tulasikā harihi priya. 5(ka).
sunu sītā tava nāma sumiri nāri pativrata karahi.
tohi prānapriya rāma kahiuṃ kathā saṃsāra hita. 5(kha).
verses.translation:
Though naturally impure, a woman attains a blessed state by serving her husband. The four Vedas sing her glory — even today, the tulasi plant is dear to Hari. ||5a||
Hear, O Sita, by remembering your name, women shall practice devotion to their husbands. You are dearest to Rama — I have told you this story for the good of the world. ||5b||

verses.original:
सुनि जानकीं परम सुखु पावा। सादर तासु चरन सिरु नावा।।
तब मुनि सन कह कृपानिधाना। आयसु होइ जाउँ बन आना।।
verses.transliteration:
suni jānakīṃ parama sukhu pāvā. sādara tāsu carana siru nāvā.
taba muni sana kaha kṛpānidhānā. āyasu hoi jāuṃ bana ānā.
verses.translation: Hearing this, Janaki felt supreme joy and reverently bowed her head at Anasuya's feet. Then the Treasure of Mercy spoke to the sage: 'Grant me leave to go to another forest.'

verses.original:
संतत मो पर कृपा करेहू। सेवक जानि तजेहु जनि नेहू।।
धर्म धुरंधर प्रभु कै बानी। सुनि सप्रेम बोले मुनि ग्यानी।।
verses.transliteration:
santata mo para kṛpā karehū. sevaka jāni tajehu jani nehū.
dharma dhurandhara prabhu kai bānī. suni saprema bole muni gyānī.
verses.translation: 'Always bestow Your grace upon me. Knowing me as Your servant, do not forsake Your affection.' Hearing the words of the Lord, the upholder of dharma, the wise sage spoke with love:

verses.original:
जासु कृपा अज सिव सनकादी। चहत सकल परमारथ बादी।।
ते तुम्ह राम अकाम पिआरे। दीन बंधु मृदु बचन उचारे।।
verses.transliteration:
jāsu kṛpā aja siva sanakādī. cahata sakala paramāratha bādī.
te tumha rāma akāma piāre. dīna bandhu mṛdu bacana ucāre.
verses.translation: 'He whose grace Brahma, Shiva, Sanaka, and all seekers of the Supreme Truth desire — that You, O Rama, are the beloved of the desireless, the friend of the humble, speaking such gentle words.'

verses.original:
अब जानी मैं श्री चतुराई। भजी तुम्हहि सब देव बिहाई।।
जेहि समान अतिसय नहिं कोई। ता कर सील कस न अस होई।।
verses.transliteration:
aba jānī maiṃ śrī caturāī. bhajī tumhahi saba deva bihāī.
jehi samāna atisaya nahiṃ koī. tā kara sīla kasa na asa hoī.
verses.translation: 'Now I have understood the wisdom of Lakshmi, who worships You, abandoning all other gods. How could one who has no equal not possess such gentle nature?'

verses.original:
केहि बिधि कहौं जाहु अब स्वामी। कहहु नाथ तुम्ह अंतरजामी।।
अस कहि प्रभु बिलोकि मुनि धीरा। लोचन जल बह पुलक सरीरा।।
verses.transliteration:
kehi bidhi kahauṃ jāhu aba svāmī. kahahu nātha tumha antarajāmī.
asa kahi prabhu biloki muni dhīrā. locana jala baha pulaka sarīrā.
verses.translation: 'How shall I tell You to go now, O Lord? You tell me, O Master, for You know all hearts.' Saying this, the steadfast sage gazed upon the Lord, tears flowing from his eyes, his body thrilling with emotion.

verses.original:
तन पुलक निर्भर प्रेम पुरन नयन मुख पंकज दिए।
मन ग्यान गुन गोतीत प्रभु मैं दीख जप तप का किए।।
जप जोग धर्म समूह तें नर भगति अनुपम पावई।
रधुबीर चरित पुनीत निसि दिन दास तुलसी गावई।।
verses.transliteration:
tana pulaka nirbhara prema purana nayana mukha paṅkaja die.
mana gyāna guṇa gotīta prabhu maiṃ dīkha japa tapa kā kie.
japa joga dharma samūha teṃ nara bhagati anupama pāvaī.
raghubīra carita punīta nisi dina dāsa tulasī gāvaī.
verses.translation: With body thrilling, overwhelmed with love, his eyes and lotus-face radiant — 'The Lord who is beyond mind, knowledge, qualities, and attributes — how have I beheld Him, what austerities or chanting have I performed? Through chanting, yoga, and religious observances one attains incomparable devotion. Day and night, the servant Tulasi sings the holy deeds of Raghubir.'

verses.original:
कलिमल समन दमन मन राम सुजस सुखमूल।
सादर सुनहि जे तिन्ह पर राम रहहिं अनुकूल।।6(क)।।
कठिन काल मल कोस धर्म न ग्यान न जोग जप।
परिहरि सकल भरोस रामहि भजहिं ते चतुर नर।।6(ख)।।
verses.transliteration:
kalimala samana damana mana rāma sujasa sukhamūla.
sādara sunahi je tinha para rāma rahahiṃ anukūla. 6(ka).
kaṭhina kāla mala kosa dharma na gyāna na joga japa.
parihari sakala bharosa rāmahi bhajahiṃ te catura nara. 6(kha).
verses.translation:
The glory of Rama, which destroys the sins of Kali age, subdues the mind, and is the root of happiness — those who hear it with reverence, Rama remains favorable to them. ||6a||
In this terrible age of Kali, a storehouse of sins, where there is neither dharma, nor wisdom, nor yoga, nor austerity — those wise persons who abandon all other reliance and worship Rama alone are truly clever. ||6b||

verses.original:
मुनि पद कमल नाइ करि सीसा। चले बनहि सुर नर मुनि ईसा।।
आगे राम अनुज पुनि पाछें। मुनि बर बेष बने अति काछें।।
verses.transliteration:
muni pada kamala nāi kari sīsā. cale banahi sura nara muni īsā.
āge rāma anuja puni pācheṃ. muni bara beṣa bane ati kācheṃ.
verses.translation: Bowing His head at the sage's lotus feet, the Lord of gods, men, and sages proceeded to the forest. Rama walked ahead, His younger brother behind, both dressed splendidly in sage's garb.

verses.original:
उमय बीच श्री सोहइ कैसी। ब्रह्म जीव बिच माया जैसी।।
सरिता बन गिरि अवघट घाटा। पति पहिचानी देहिं बर बाटा।।
verses.transliteration:
umaya bīca śrī sohai kaisī. brahma jīva bica māyā jaisī.
saritā bana giri avaghaṭa ghāṭā. pati pahicānī dehiṃ bara bāṭā.
verses.translation: Between the two, Sita shone like Maya between Brahman and the individual soul. Rivers, forests, mountains, and difficult passes — recognizing their Lord, they offered Him pleasant paths.

verses.original:
जहँ जहँ जाहि देव रघुराया। करहिं मेध तहँ तहँ नभ छाया।।
मिला असुर बिराध मग जाता। आवतहीं रघुवीर निपाता।।
verses.transliteration:
jahaṃ jahaṃ jāhi deva raghurāyā. karahiṃ megha tahaṃ tahaṃ nabha chāyā.
milā asura birādha maga jātā. āvatahīṃ raghuvīra nipātā.
verses.translation: Wherever the divine Lord of the Raghus went, clouds provided shade in the sky. On the way they encountered the demon Viradha, whom Lord Rama slew as soon as he appeared.

verses.original:
तुरतहिं रुचिर रूप तेहिं पावा। देखि दुखी निज धाम पठावा।।
पुनि आए जहँ मुनि सरभंगा। सुंदर अनुज जानकी संगा।।
verses.transliteration:
turatahiṃ rucira rūpa tehi pāvā. dekhi dukhī nija dhāma paṭhāvā.
puni āe jahaṃ muni sarabhangā. sundara anuja jānakī saṅgā.
verses.translation: Immediately, the demon received a beautiful divine form. Seeing his distress, the Lord sent him to His own abode. Then they came to where the sage Sharabhanga dwelt, accompanied by His beautiful brother and Janaki.

verses.original:
देखी राम मुख पंकज मुनिबर लोचन भृंग।
सादर पान करत अति धन्य जन्म सरभंग।।7।।
verses.transliteration:
dekhī rāma mukha paṅkaja munibara locana bhṛṅga.
sādara pāna karata ati dhanya janma sarabhanga. 7.
verses.translation: Seeing Rama's lotus face, the bee-like eyes of the great sage Sharabhanga reverently drank their fill. Blessed was the birth of Sharabhanga! ||7||

verses.original:
कह मुनि सुनु रघुबीर कृपाला। संकर मानस राजमराला।।
जात रहेउँ बिरंचि के धामा। सुनेउँ श्रवन बन ऐहहिं रामा।।
verses.transliteration:
kaha muni sunu raghubīra kṛpālā. śaṅkara mānasa rājamarālā.
jāta raheuṃ birañci ke dhāmā. suneuṃ śravana bana aihahiṃ rāmā.
verses.translation: The sage said: 'Listen, O gracious Raghubir, O royal swan on the Manasa lake of Shankara's heart. I was going to Brahma's abode when I heard that Rama would come to the forest.'

verses.original:
चितवत पंथ रहेउँ दिन राती। अब प्रभु देखि जुड़ानी छाती।।
नाथ सकल साधन मैं हीना। कीन्ही कृपा जानि जन दीना।।
verses.transliteration:
citavata pantha raheuṃ dina rātī. aba prabhu dekhi juḍānī chātī.
nātha sakala sādhana maiṃ hīnā. kīnhī kṛpā jāni jana dīnā.
verses.translation: 'I kept watching the path day and night. Now, Lord, seeing You, my heart is cooled. O Master, I am devoid of all spiritual practices; You have shown me grace knowing me to be a humble devotee.'

verses.original:
सो कछु देव न मोहि निहोरा। निज पन राखेउ जन मन चोरा।।
तब लगि रहहु दीन हित लागी। जब लगि मिलौं तुम्हहि तनु त्यागी।।
verses.transliteration:
so kachu deva na mohi nihorā. nija pana rākheu jana mana corā.
taba lagi rahahu dīna hita lāgī. jaba lagi milauṃ tumhahi tanu tyāgī.
verses.translation: 'That grace, O Lord, is no obligation upon me — You have merely kept Your own vow, O stealer of devotees' hearts. Stay here, O Lord, for the sake of the humble, until I meet You again after leaving this body.'

verses.original:
जोग जग्य जप तप ब्रत कीन्हा। प्रभु कहँ देइ भगति बर लीन्हा।।
एहि बिधि सर रचि मुनि सरभंगा। बैठे हृदयँ छाड़ि सब संगा।।
verses.transliteration:
joga jagya japa tapa brata kīnhā. prabhu kahaṃ dei bhagati bara līnhā.
ehi bidhi sara raci muni sarabhangā. baiṭhe hṛdayaṃ chāḍi saba saṅgā.
verses.translation: He offered all his spiritual merit from chanting, sacrifices, austerity, and vows to the Lord, and received the boon of devotion. In this manner, the sage Sharabhanga prepared his funeral pyre and sat upon it, abandoning all attachments in his heart.

verses.original:
सीता अनुज समेत प्रभु नील जलद तनु स्याम।
मम हियँ बसहु निरंतर सगुनरुप श्रीराम।।8।।
verses.transliteration:
sītā anuja sameta prabhu nīla jalada tanu syāma.
mama hiyaṃ basahu nirantara sagunarūpa śrīrāma. 8.
verses.translation: May Lord Rama, with Sita and His younger brother, dark-hued like a blue cloud, dwell forever in my heart in His manifest form! ||8||

verses.original:
अस कहि जोग अगिनि तनु जारा। राम कृपाँ बैकुंठ सिधारा।।
ताते मुनि हरि लीन न भयऊ। प्रथमहिं भेद भगति बर लयऊ।।
verses.transliteration:
asa kahi joga agini tanu jārā. rāma kṛpāṃ baikuṇṭha sidhārā.
tāte muni hari līna na bhayaū. prathamahi bheda bhagati bara layaū.
verses.translation: Saying this, he burned his body in the fire of yoga and, by Rama's grace, went to Vaikuntha. Therefore the sage did not merge into the formless Absolute — he had already received the boon of distinct devotion.

verses.original:
रिषि निकाय मुनिबर गति देखि। सुखी भए निज हृदयँ बिसेषी।।
अस्तुति करहिं सकल मुनि बृंदा। जयति प्रनत हित करुना कंदा।।
verses.transliteration:
riṣi nikāya munibara gati dekhī. sukhī bhae nija hṛdayaṃ biseṣī.
astuti karahiṃ sakala muni bṛndā. jayati pranata hita karunā kandā.
verses.translation: Seeing the great fate of the sage, all the assembly of sages became especially happy in their hearts. All the groups of sages began to praise: 'Victory to the Compassionate One, the friend of the suppliant!'

verses.original:
पुनि रघुनाथ चले बन आगे। मुनिबर बृंद बिपुल सँग लागे।।
अस्थि समूह देखि रघुराया। पूछी मुनिन्ह लागि अति दाया।।
verses.transliteration:
puni raghunātha cale bana āge. munibara bṛnda bipula saṃga lāge.
asthi samūha dekhi raghurāyā. pūchī muninh lāgi ati dāyā.
verses.translation: Then the Lord of the Raghus proceeded further into the forest. Many groups of sages followed Him. Seeing heaps of bones, the Lord of the Raghus asked the sages, moved by great compassion.

verses.original:
जानतहुँ पूछिअ कस स्वामी। सबदरसी तुम्ह अंतरजामी।।
निसिचर निकर सकल मुनि खाए। सुनि रघुबीर नयन जल छाए।।
verses.transliteration:
jānatahūṃ pūchiahiṃ kasa svāmī. sabadarasī tumha antarajāmī.
nisicara nikara sakala muni khāe. suni raghubīra nayana jala chāe.
verses.translation: 'Why do You ask, O Lord, when You already know? You are all-seeing, the inner knower of all hearts.' 'Hordes of demons have devoured all the sages.' Hearing this, tears welled in Raghubir's eyes.

verses.original:
निसिचर हीन करउँ महि भुज उठाइ पन कीन्ह।
सकल मुनिन्ह के आश्रमन्हि जाइ जाइ सुख दीन्ह।।9।।
verses.transliteration:
nisicara hīna karauṃ mahi bhuja uṭhāi pana kīnha.
sakala muninh ke āśramanh jāi jāi sukha dīnha. 9.
verses.translation: 'I shall rid the earth of demons!' — raising His arm, He made this vow. He then went to the hermitages of all the sages and brought them comfort. ||9||

verses.original:
मुनि अगस्ति कर सिष्य सुजाना। नाम सुतीछन रति भगवाना।।
मन क्रम बचन राम पद सेवक। सपनेहुँ आन भरोस न देवक।।
verses.transliteration:
muni agasti kara siṣya sujānā. nāma sutīchana rati bhagavānā.
mana krama bacana rāma pada sevaka. sapanehuṃ āna bharosa na devaka.
verses.translation: A wise disciple of sage Agastya, named Sutikshna, was full of love for the Lord. In thought, deed, and word a servant of Rama's feet, he would not even dream of placing trust in anyone else.

verses.original:
प्रभु आगवनु श्रवन सुनि पावा। करत मनोरथ आतुर धावा।।
हे बिधि दीनबंधु रघुराया। मो से सठ पर करिहहिं दाया।।
verses.transliteration:
prabhu āgavanu śravana suni pāvā. karata manoratha ātura dhāvā.
he bidhi dīnabandhu raghurāyā. mo se saṭha para karihahiṃ dāyā.
verses.translation: When he heard that the Lord was coming, he made many wishes and ran forward eagerly: 'O Providence! Will the compassionate Lord of the Raghus, friend of the humble, show mercy to a wretch like me?'

verses.original:
सहित अनुज मोहि राम गोसाई। मिलिहहिं निज सेवक की नाई।।
मोरे जियँ भरोस दृढ़ नाहीं। भगति बिरति न ग्यान मन माहीं।।
verses.transliteration:
sahita anuja mohi rāma gosāī. milihahiṃ nija sevaka kī nāī.
more jiyaṃ bharosa dṛḍha nāhīṃ. bhagati birati na gyāna mana māhīṃ.
verses.translation: 'Will Lord Rama, together with His brother, meet me as His own servant? My heart has no firm conviction — there is neither devotion, nor detachment, nor wisdom in my mind.'

verses.original:
नहिं सतसंग जोग जप जागा। नहिं दृढ़ चरन कमल अनुरागा।।
एक बानि करुनानिधान की। सो प्रिय जाकें गति न आन की।।
verses.transliteration:
nahiṃ satasaṅga joga japa jāgā. nahiṃ dṛḍha carana kamala anurāgā.
eka bāni karunānidāna kī. so priya jākeṃ gati na āna kī.
verses.translation: 'There is no holy company, nor yoga, nor chanting, nor awakening; nor is there firm love for His lotus feet. But the Compassionate One has this one trait: He holds dear those who have no other refuge.'

verses.original:
होइहैं सुफल आजु मम लोचन। देखि बदन पंकज भव मोचन।।
निर्भर प्रेम मगन मुनि ग्यानी। कहि न जाइ सो दसा भवानी।।
verses.transliteration:
hoihai suphala āju mama locana. dekhi badana paṅkaja bhava mocana.
nirbhara prema magana muni gyānī. kahi na jāi so dasā bhavānī.
verses.translation: 'Today my eyes shall bear fruit, beholding that lotus face which liberates from worldly existence.' Overwhelmed with intense love, the wise sage was in a state, O Bhavani, that cannot be described.

verses.original:
दिसि अरु बिदिसि पंथ नहिं सूझा। को मैं चलेउँ कहाँ नहिं बूझा।।
कबहुँक फिरि पाछें पुनि जाई। कबहुँक नृत्य करइ गुन गाई।।
verses.transliteration:
disi aru bidisi pantha nahiṃ sūjhā. ko maiṃ caleuṃ kahāṃ nahiṃ būjhā.
kabahūṃka phiri pācheṃ puni jāī. kabahūṃka nṛtya karai guna gāī.
verses.translation: He could not tell direction from non-direction, nor the path; he did not know who he was or where he was going. Sometimes he would turn back, and sometimes he would dance, singing the Lord's glories.

verses.original:
अबिरल प्रेम भगति मुनि पाई। प्रभु देखैं तरु ओट लुकाई।।
अतिसय प्रीति देखि रघुबीरा। प्रगटे हृदयँ हरन भव भीरा।।
verses.transliteration:
abirala prema bhagati muni pāī. prabhu dekhaiṃ taru oṭa lukāī.
atisaya prīti dekhi raghubīrā. pragaṭe hṛdayaṃ harana bhava bhīrā.
verses.translation: The sage attained unbroken love-devotion. Seeing him, the Lord hid behind a tree. Beholding his extraordinary love, Raghubir, the remover of the fear of worldly existence, manifested in his heart.

verses.original:
मुनि मग माझ अचल होइ बैसा। पुलक सरीर पनस फल जैसा।।
तब रघुनाथ निकट चलि आए। देखि दसा निज जन मन भाए।।
verses.transliteration:
muni maga mājha acala hoi baisā. pulaka sarīra panasa phala jaisā.
taba raghunātha nikaṭa cali āe. dekhi dasā nija jana mana bhāe.
verses.translation: The sage sat motionless in the middle of the road, his body thrilling like a jackfruit. Then the Lord of the Raghus drew near, pleased to see the state of His devotee.

verses.original:
मुनिहि राम बहु भाँति जगावा। जाग न ध्यानजनित सुख पावा।।
भूप रूप तब राम दुरावा। हृदयँ चतुर्भुज रूप देखावा।।
verses.transliteration:
munihi rāma bahu bhāṃti jagāvā. jāga na dhyānajanita sukha pāvā.
bhūpa rūpa taba rāma durāvā. hṛdayaṃ caturbhuja rūpa dekhāvā.
verses.translation: Rama tried in many ways to rouse the sage, but he would not awaken, having attained the bliss of meditation. The Lord then concealed His princely form and revealed His four-armed form in the sage's heart.

verses.original:
मुनि अकुलाइ उठा तब कैसें। बिकल हीन मनि फनि बर जैसें।।
आगें देखि राम तन स्यामा। सीता अनुज सहित सुख धामा।।
verses.transliteration:
muni akulāi uṭhā taba kaiseṃ. bikala hīna mani phani bara jaiseṃ.
āgeṃ dekhi rāma tana syāmā. sītā anuja sahita sukha dhāmā.
verses.translation: The sage then arose in agitation, like a cobra that has lost its jewel. Before him he saw Rama's dark-hued form, with Sita and His younger brother, the abode of bliss.

verses.original:
परेउ लकुट इव चरनन्हि लागी। प्रेम मगन मुनिबर बड़भागी।।
भुज बिसाल गहि लिए उठाई। परम प्रीति राखे उर लाई।।
verses.transliteration:
pareu lakuṭa iva carananhi lāgī. prema magana munibara baḍabhāgī.
bhuja bisāla gahi lie uṭhāī. parama prīti rākhe ura lāī.
verses.translation: He fell at the Lord's feet like a stick. The most fortunate sage was immersed in love. With His mighty arms, the Lord raised him up and held him close to His heart with supreme affection.

verses.original:
मुनिहि मिलत अस सोह कृपाला। कनक तरुहि जनु भेंट तमाला।।
राम बदनु बिलोक मुनि ठाढ़ा। मानहुँ चित्र माझ लिखि काढ़ा।।
verses.transliteration:
munihi milata asa soha kṛpālā. kanaka taruhi janu bheṃṭa tamālā.
rāma badanu biloka muni ṭhāḍhā. mānahuṃ citra mājha likhi kāḍhā.
verses.translation: Meeting the sage, the Merciful One looked as beautiful as a tamala tree embracing a golden tree. The sage stood gazing at Rama's face as if he were a portrait drawn in a painting.

verses.original:
तब मुनि हृदयँ धीर धीर गहि पद बारहिं बार।
निज आश्रम प्रभु आनि करि पूजा बिबिध प्रकार।।10।।
verses.transliteration:
taba muni hṛdayaṃ dhīra dhīra gahi pada bārahiṃ bāra.
nija āśrama prabhu āni kari pūjā bibidha prakāra. 10.
verses.translation: Then the sage, composing himself with patience, clasped the Lord's feet again and again and brought the Lord to his hermitage, worshipping Him in various ways. ||10||

verses.original:
कह मुनि प्रभु सुनु बिनती मोरी। अस्तुति करौं कवन बिधि तोरी।।
महिमा अमित मोरि मति थोरी। रबि सन्मुख खद्योत अँजोरी।।
verses.transliteration:
kaha muni prabhu sunu binatī morī. astuti karauṃ kavana bidhi torī.
mahimā amita mori mati thorī. rabi sanmukha khadyota aṃjorī.
verses.translation: The sage said: 'Lord, hear my plea — in what manner can I praise You? Your glory is infinite and my intellect is small — like a firefly's light before the sun.'

verses.original:
श्याम तामरस दाम शरीरं। जटा मुकुट परिधन मुनिचीरं।।
पाणि चाप शर कटि तूणीरं। नौमि निरंतर श्रीरघुवीरं।।
verses.transliteration:
śyāma tāmarasa dāma śarīraṃ. jaṭā mukuṭa paridhana munichīraṃ.
pāṇi cāpa śara kaṭi tūṇīraṃ. naumi nirantara śrīraghuvīraṃ.
verses.translation: 'I bow to Sri Raghuvir — dark-bodied like a garland of blue lotuses, wearing a crown of matted hair and clad in sage's bark. With bow and arrow in hand and quiver at His waist, I ceaselessly bow to Sri Raghuvir.'

verses.original:
मोह विपिन घन दहन कृशानुः। संत सरोरुह कानन भानुः।।
निशिचर करि वरूथ मृगराजः। त्रातु सदा नो भव खग बाजः।।
verses.transliteration:
moha vipina ghana dahana kṛśānuḥ. santa saroruha kānana bhānuḥ.
nisicara kari varūtha mṛgarājaḥ. trātu sadā no bhava khaga bājaḥ.
verses.translation: 'He is the fire that burns the dense forest of delusion; the sun for the lotus-garden of saints. He is the lion against the elephant-herd of demons. May that falcon of the sky of worldly existence always protect us!'

verses.original:
अरुण नयन राजीव सुवेशं। सीता नयन चकोर निशेशं।।
हर ह्रदि मानस बाल मरालं। नौमि राम उर बाहु विशालं।।
verses.transliteration:
aruṇa nayana rājīva suveśaṃ. sītā nayana cakora niśeśaṃ.
hara hrdi mānasa bāla marālaṃ. naumi rāma ura bāhu viśālaṃ.
verses.translation: 'With red lotus eyes, beautifully attired, the full moon for the chakora bird of Sita's eyes. Like a young swan on the Manasa lake of Shiva's heart — I bow to Rama, broad of chest and mighty of arm.'

verses.original:
संशय सर्प ग्रसन उरगादः। शमन सुकर्कश तर्क विषादः।।
भव भंजन रंजन सुर यूथः। त्रातु सदा नो कृपा वरूथः।।
verses.transliteration:
saṃśaya sarpa grasana uragādaḥ. śamana sukarkaśa tarka viṣādaḥ.
bhava bhañjana rañjana sura yūthaḥ. trātu sadā no kṛpā varūthaḥ.
verses.translation: 'He is Garuda who devours the serpent of doubt; the destroyer, more terrible than the sharpest logic. Destroyer of worldly existence, delighter of hosts of gods — may He, our chariot of mercy, always protect us!'

verses.original:
निर्गुण सगुण विषम सम रूपं। ज्ञान गिरा गोतीतमनूपं।।
अमलमखिलमनवद्यमपारं। नौमि राम भंजन महि भारं।।
verses.transliteration:
nirguṇa saguṇa viṣama sama rūpaṃ. jñāna girā gotītamanūpaṃ.
amalamakhilamanavadyamapāraṃ. naumi rāma bhañjana mahi bhāraṃ.
verses.translation: 'Formless and with form, unequal yet equal in nature, beyond knowledge and speech, incomparable. Spotless, complete, faultless, infinite — I bow to Rama, the remover of earth's burden.'

verses.original:
भक्त कल्पपादप आरामः। तर्जन क्रोध लोभ मद कामः।।
अति नागर भव सागर सेतुः। त्रातु सदा दिनकर कुल केतुः।।
verses.transliteration:
bhakta kalpapādapa ārāmaḥ. tarjana krodha lobha mada kāmaḥ.
ati nāgara bhava sāgara setuḥ. trātu sadā dinakara kula ketuḥ.
verses.translation: 'A garden of wish-fulfilling trees for devotees; the vanquisher of anger, greed, pride, and lust. Most skillful, the bridge across the ocean of worldly existence — may the banner of the Solar dynasty always protect us!'

verses.original:
अतुलित भुज प्रताप बल धामः। कलि मल विपुल विभंजन नामः।।
धर्म वर्म नर्मद गुण ग्रामः। संतत शं तनोतु मम रामः।।
verses.transliteration:
atulita bhuja pratāpa bala dhāmaḥ. kali mala vipula vibhañjana nāmaḥ.
dharma varma narmada guṇa grāmaḥ. santata śaṃ tanotu mama rāmaḥ.
verses.translation: 'He whose arms possess unmatched valor and strength; whose Name destroys the great sins of the Kali age. He who is the armor of dharma, delightful, and the abode of all virtues — may Rama always bestow blessings upon me!'

verses.original:
जदपि बिरज ब्यापक अबिनासी। सब के हृदयँ निरंतर बासी।।
तदपि अनुज श्री सहित खरारी। बसतु मनसि मम काननचारी।।
verses.transliteration:
jadapi biraja byāpaka abināsī. saba ke hṛdayaṃ nirantara bāsī.
tadapi anuja śrī sahita kharārī. basatu manasi mama kānanacārī.
verses.translation: 'Though He is pure, all-pervading, and imperishable, dwelling eternally in everyone's heart, yet may the slayer of Khara — with His brother and Lakshmi, wandering the forest — dwell in my mind.'

verses.original:
जे जानहिं ते जानहुँ स्वामी। सगुन अगुन उर अंतरजामी।।
जो कोसल पति राजिव नयना। करउ सो राम हृदय मम अयना।
verses.transliteration:
je jānahiṃ te jānahuṃ svāmī. saguna aguna ura antarajāmī.
jo kosala pati rājiva nayanā. karau so rāma hṛdaya mama ayanā.
verses.translation: 'Those who know, know You, O Master, the inner controller of the heart, both with form and formless. May that Rama who is the lotus-eyed Lord of Kosala make my heart His abode.'

verses.original:
अस अभिमान जाइ जनि भोरे। मैं सेवक रघुपति पति मोरे।।
सुनि मुनि बचन राम मन भाए। बहुरि हरषि मुनिबर उर लाए।।
verses.transliteration:
asa abhimāna jāi jani bhore. maiṃ sevaka raghupati pati more.
suni muni bacana rāma mana bhāe. bahuri haraṣi munibara ura lāe.
verses.translation: 'Let not this pride slip away inadvertently — I am the servant, and Raghupati is my master.' Hearing the sage's words, Rama was pleased and joyfully embraced the sage again.

verses.original:
परम प्रसन्न जानु मुनि मोही। जो बर मागहु देउ सो तोही।।
मुनि कह मै बर कबहुँ न जाचा। समुझि न परइ झूठ का साचा।।
verses.transliteration:
parama prasanna jānu muni mohī. jo bara māgahu deu so tohī.
muni kaha mai bara kabahūṃ na jācā. samujhi na parai jhūṭha kā sācā.
verses.translation: 'Know Me to be most pleased with you, O sage. Whatever boon you ask, I shall grant it.' The sage replied: 'I have never asked for a boon; I cannot tell what is false from what is true.'

verses.original:
तुम्हहि नीक लागै रघुराई। सो मोहि देहु दास सुखदाई।।
अबिरल भगति बिरति बिग्याना। होहु सकल गुन ग्यान निधाना।।
verses.transliteration:
tumhahi nīka lāgai raghurāī. so mohi dehu dāsa sukhadāī.
abirala bhagati birati bigyānā. hohu sakala guṇa gyāna nidhānā.
verses.translation: 'Whatever pleases You, O Lord of the Raghus, give me that, O joy-giver of Your servants. Unbroken devotion, detachment, and spiritual wisdom — be the treasury of all virtues and knowledge.'

verses.original: प्रभु जो दीन्ह सो बरु मैं पावा। अब सो देहु मोहि जो भावा।।
verses.transliteration: prabhu jo dīnha so baru maiṃ pāvā. aba so dehu mohi jo bhāvā.
verses.translation: 'The boon You have given, Lord, I have already received. Now grant me what I truly desire.'

verses.original:
अनुज जानकी सहित प्रभु चाप बान धर राम।
मम हिय गगन इंदु इव बसहु सदा निहकाम।।11।।
verses.transliteration:
anuja jānakī sahita prabhu cāpa bāna dhara rāma.
mama hiya gagana indu iva basahu sadā nihakāma. 11.
verses.translation: May the Lord Rama — with His brother, Janaki, bow, and arrows — dwell in the sky of my heart forever like the moon, free from all desires. ||11||

verses.original:
एवमस्तु करि रमानिवासा। हरषि चले कुभंज रिषि पासा।।
बहुत दिवस गुर दरसन पाएँ। भए मोहि एहिं आश्रम आएँ।।
verses.transliteration:
evamastu kari ramānivāsā. haraṣi cale kubhanja riṣi pāsā.
bahuta divasa gura darasana pāeṃ. bhae mohi ehiṃ āśrama āeṃ.
verses.translation: 'So be it,' said the abode of Lakshmi, and He joyfully proceeded to the sage Agastya (Kumbhaja). 'It has been many days since I had the sight of my guru; I have been staying at this hermitage.'

verses.original:
अब प्रभु संग जाउँ गुर पाहीं। तुम्ह कहँ नाथ निहोरा नाहीं।।
देखि कृपानिधि मुनि चतुराई। लिए संग बिहसै द्वौ भाई।।
verses.transliteration:
aba prabhu saṅga jāuṃ gura pāhīṃ. tumha kahaṃ nātha nihorā nāhīṃ.
dekhi kṛpānidhi muni caturāī. lie saṅga bihasai dvau bhāī.
verses.translation: 'Now I shall go with You to my guru. For You, Lord, there is no obligation.' Seeing the sage's cleverness, the Compassionate One took him along, and the two brothers smiled.

verses.original:
पंथ कहत निज भगति अनूपा। मुनि आश्रम पहुँचे सुरभूपा।।
तुरत सुतीछन गुर पहिं गयऊ। करि दंडवत कहत अस भयऊ।।
verses.transliteration:
pantha kahata nija bhagati anūpā. muni āśrama pahūṃce surabhūpā.
turata sutīchana gura pahiṃ gayaū. kari daṇḍavata kahata asa bhayaū.
verses.translation: Along the way, describing His own incomparable devotion, the Lord of the gods reached the sage's hermitage. Sutikshna immediately went to his guru and, prostrating, spoke thus:

verses.original:
नाथ कौसलाधीस कुमारा। आए मिलन जगत आधारा।।
राम अनुज समेत बैदेही। निसि दिनु देव जपत हहु जेही।।
verses.transliteration:
nātha kausalādhīsa kumārā. āe milana jagata ādhārā.
rāma anuja sameta baidehī. nisi dinu deva japata hahu jehī.
verses.translation: 'Master, the princes of the King of Kosala have come to meet you — the support of the universe. Rama with His brother and Vaidehi — the One whom you worship day and night.'

verses.original:
सुनत अगस्ति तुरत उठि धाए। हरि बिलोकि लोचन जल छाए।।
मुनि पद कमल परे द्वौ भाई। रिषि अति प्रीति लिए उर लाई।।
verses.transliteration:
sunata agasti turata uṭhi dhāe. hari biloki locana jala chāe.
muni pada kamala pare dvau bhāī. riṣi ati prīti lie ura lāī.
verses.translation: Hearing this, Agastya instantly rose and ran. Seeing Hari, his eyes overflowed with tears. The two brothers fell at the sage's lotus feet; the sage, overwhelmed with love, clasped them to his heart.

verses.original:
सादर कुसल पूछि मुनि ग्यानी। आसन बर बैठारे आनी।।
पुनि करि बहु प्रकार प्रभु पूजा। मोहि सम भाग्यवंत नहिं दूजा।।
verses.transliteration:
sādara kusala pūchi muni gyānī. āsana bara baiṭhāre ānī.
puni kari bahu prakāra prabhu pūjā. mohi sama bhāgyavanta nahiṃ dūjā.
verses.translation: The wise sage respectfully asked after their welfare and brought fine seats for them to sit upon. Then, worshipping the Lord in many ways, he said: 'None is as fortunate as I.'

verses.original: जहँ लगि रहे अपर मुनि बृंदा। हरषे सब बिलोकि सुखकंदा।।
verses.transliteration: jahaṃ lagi rahe apara muni bṛndā. haraṣe saba biloki sukhakandā.
verses.translation: As many other groups of sages were there, all rejoiced beholding the Source of Bliss.

verses.original:
मुनि समूह महँ बैठे सन्मुख सब की ओर।
सरद इंदु तन चितवत मानहुँ निकर चकोर।।12।।
verses.transliteration:
muni samūha mahaṃ baiṭhe sanmukha saba kī ora.
sarada indu tana citavata mānahuṃ nikara cakora. 12.
verses.translation: Seated among the assembly of sages, facing them all, He appeared like the autumn moon at which a multitude of chakora birds gaze. ||12||

verses.original:
तब रघुबीर कहा मुनि पाहीं। तुम्ह सन प्रभु दुराव कछु नाही।।
तुम्ह जानहु जेहि कारन आयउँ। ताते तात न कहि समुझायउँ।।
verses.transliteration:
taba raghubīra kahā muni pāhīṃ. tumha sana prabhu durāva kachu nāhī.
tumha jānahu jehi kārana āyauṃ. tāte tāta na kahi samujhāyauṃ.
verses.translation: Then Raghubir said to the sage: 'Nothing is hidden from you, Lord. You know the purpose for which I have come; therefore, dear one, I need not explain.'

verses.original:
अब सो मंत्र देहु प्रभु मोही। जेहि प्रकार मारौं मुनिद्रोही।।
मुनि मुसकाने सुनि प्रभु बानी। पूछेहु नाथ मोहि का जानी।।
verses.transliteration:
aba so mantra dehu prabhu mohī. jehi prakāra mārauṃ munidrohī.
muni musakāne suni prabhu bānī. pūchehu nātha mohi kā jānī.
verses.translation: 'Now give me counsel, O sage, by which I may slay the tormentors of the sages.' The sage smiled upon hearing the Lord's words: 'You ask me, Lord — what do I know?'

verses.original:
तुम्हरेइँ भजन प्रभाव अघारी। जानउँ महिमा कछुक तुम्हारी।।
ऊमरि तरु बिसाल तव माया। फल ब्रह्मांड अनेक निकाया।।
verses.transliteration:
tumhareīṃ bhajana prabhāva aghārī. jānauṃ mahimā kachuka tumhārī.
ūmari taru bisāla tava māyā. phala brahmāṇḍa aneka nikāyā.
verses.translation: 'By the power of worshipping You, O destroyer of sins, I know some measure of Your glory. Your Maya is like a vast fig tree, whose fruits are the innumerable universes.'

verses.original:
जीव चराचर जंतु समाना। भीतर बसहि न जानहिं आना।।
ते फल भच्छक कठिन कराला। तव भयँ डरत सदा सोउ काला।।
verses.transliteration:
jīva carācara jantu samānā. bhītara basahi na jānahi ānā.
te phala bhacchaka kaṭhina karālā. tava bhayaṃ ḍarata sadā sou kālā.
verses.translation: 'All beings, moving and unmoving, dwell within those fruits, knowing nothing else. The devourers of those fruits (Time) are terrifying — and even that Time is ever afraid of You.'

verses.original:
ते तुम्ह सकल लोकपति साईं। पूँछेहु मोहि मनुज की नाईं।।
यह बर मागउँ कृपानिकेता। बसहु हृदयँ श्री अनुज समेता।।
verses.transliteration:
te tumha sakala lokapati sāīṃ. pūṃchehu mohi manuja kī nāīṃ.
yaha bara māgauṃ kṛpāniketā. basahu hṛdayaṃ śrī anuja sametā.
verses.translation: 'You are the Lord of all the worlds, yet You ask me as if You were a mere mortal. This boon I ask, O abode of compassion: dwell in my heart with Lakshmi and Your younger brother.'

verses.original:
अबिरल भगति बिरति सतसंगा। चरन सरोरुह प्रीति अभंगा।।
जद्यपि ब्रह्म अखंड अनंता। अनुभव गम्य भजहिं जेहि संता।।
verses.transliteration:
abirala bhagati birati satasaṅgā. carana saroruha prīti abhaṅgā.
jadyapi brahma akhaṇḍa anantā. anubhava gamya bhajahiṃ jehi santā.
verses.translation: 'Unbroken devotion, detachment, and holy company; unbreakable love for Your lotus feet. Though the Brahman is undivided and infinite, experienced directly, whom the saints worship —'

verses.original:
अस तव रूप बखानउँ जानउँ। फिरि फिरि सगुन ब्रह्म रति मानउँ।।
संतत दासन्ह देहु बड़ाई। तातें मोहि पूँछेहु रघुराई।।
verses.transliteration:
asa tava rūpa bakhānauṃ jānauṃ. phiri phiri saguna brahma rati mānauṃ.
santata dāsanh dehu baḍāī. tāteṃ mohi pūṃchehu raghurāī.
verses.translation: '— such is Your form that I describe as I know it. Again and again, I delight in the manifest Brahman with form. You always exalt Your servants — that is why You have asked me, O Lord of the Raghus.'

verses.original:
है प्रभु परम मनोहर ठाऊँ। पावन पंचबटी तेहि नाऊँ।।
दंडक बन पुनीत प्रभु करहू। उग्र साप मुनिबर कर हरहू।।
verses.transliteration:
hai prabhu parama manohara ṭhāūṃ. pāvana pañcabaṭī tehi nāūṃ.
daṇḍaka bana punīta prabhu karahū. ugra sāpa munibara kara harahū.
verses.translation: 'There is a most beautiful and sacred spot called Panchavati, O Lord. Purify the Dandaka forest and remove the terrible curse of the sages.'

verses.original:
बास करहु तहँ रघुकुल राया। कीजे सकल मुनिन्ह पर दाया।।
चले राम मुनि आयसु पाई। तुरतहिं पंचबटी निअराई।।
verses.transliteration:
bāsa karahu tahaṃ raghukula rāyā. kīje sakala muninh para dāyā.
cale rāma muni āyasu pāī. turatahīṃ pañcabaṭī niarāī.
verses.translation: 'Dwell there, O King of the Raghu dynasty. Show mercy to all the sages.' Receiving the sage's command, Rama set out and soon drew near to Panchavati.

verses.original:
गीधराज सैं भैंट भइ बहु बिधि प्रीति बढ़ाइ।।
गोदावरी निकट प्रभु रहे परन गृह छाइ।।13।।
verses.transliteration:
gīdharāja saiṃ bhaiṃṭa bhaī bahu bidhi prīti baḍhāi.
godāvarī nikaṭa prabhu rahe parana gṛha chāi. 13.
verses.translation: There He met the king of vultures (Jatayu) and formed a deep bond of friendship. Near the Godavari river, the Lord made His leaf-thatched dwelling. ||13||

verses.original:
जब ते राम कीन्ह तहँ बासा। सुखी भए मुनि बीती त्रासा।।
गिरि बन नदीं ताल छबि छाए। दिन दिन प्रति अति हौहिं सुहाए।।
verses.transliteration:
jaba te rāma kīnha tahaṃ bāsā. sukhī bhae muni bītī trāsā.
giri bana nadīṃ tāla chabi chāe. dina dina prati ati hauhi suhāe.
verses.translation: Ever since Rama made His abode there, the sages became happy, their fears dispelled. Mountains, forests, rivers, and lakes became increasingly beautiful with each passing day.

verses.original:
खग मृग बृंद अनंदित रहहीं। मधुप मधुर गंजत छबि लहहीं।।
सो बन बरनि न सक अहिराजा। जहाँ प्रगट रघुबीर बिराजा।।
verses.transliteration:
khaga mṛga bṛnda anandita rahahīṃ. madhupa madhura gañjata chabi lahahīṃ.
so bana barani na saka ahirājā. jahāṃ pragaṭa raghubīra birājā.
verses.translation: Flocks of birds and herds of deer rejoiced; bees hummed sweetly, adding to the beauty. Even the serpent-king (Shesha) could not describe that forest where the great Lord Raghubir resided.

verses.original:
एक बार प्रभु सुख आसीना। लछिमन बचन कहे छलहीना।।
सुर नर मुनि सचराचर साईं। मैं पूछउँ निज प्रभु की नाई।।
verses.transliteration:
eka bāra prabhu sukha āsīnā. lacchimana bacana kahe chalahīnā.
sura nara muni sacarācara sāīṃ. maiṃ pūchauṃ nija prabhu kī nāī.
verses.translation: Once, when the Lord was seated at ease, Lakshmana spoke guileless words: 'O Lord of gods, men, sages, and all creation — I ask You as my own Lord.'

verses.original:
मोहि समुझाइ कहहु सोइ देवा। सब तजि करौं चरन रज सेवा।।
कहहु ग्यान बिराग अरु माया। कहहु सो भगति करहु जेहिं दाया।।
verses.transliteration:
mohi samujhāi kahahu soi devā. saba taji karauṃ carana raja sevā.
kahahu gyāna birāga aru māyā. kahahu so bhagati karahu jehiṃ dāyā.
verses.translation: 'Explain to me, O Lord, that by which I may abandon all else and serve the dust of Your feet. Tell me of knowledge, detachment, and Maya; tell me of that devotion on which You bestow grace.'

verses.original:
ईस्वर जीव भेद प्रभु सकल कहौ समुझाइ।।
जातें होइ चरन रति सोक मोह भ्रम जाइ।।14।।
verses.transliteration:
īsvara jīva bheda prabhu sakala kahau samujhāi.
jāteṃ hoi carana rati soka moha bhrama jāi. 14.
verses.translation: 'O Lord, explain the difference between God and the individual soul. From that understanding may devotion to Your feet arise, and may sorrow, delusion, and confusion depart.' ||14||

verses.original:
थोरेहि महँ सब कहउँ बुझाई। सुनहु तात मति मन चित लाई।।
मैं अरु मोर तोर तैं माया। जेहिं बस कीन्हे जीव निकाया।।
verses.transliteration:
thorehi mahaṃ saba kahauṃ bujhāī. sunahu tāta mati mana cita lāī.
maiṃ aru mora tora taiṃ māyā. jehiṃ basa kīnhe jīva nikāyā.
verses.translation: 'I shall explain everything briefly. Listen attentively, dear brother, with mind and heart focused. "I and mine, you and yours" — this is Maya, which has enslaved all living beings.'

verses.original:
गो गोचर जहँ लगि मन जाई। सो सब माया जानेहु भाई।।
तेहि कर भेद सुनहु तुम्ह सोऊ। बिद्या अपर अबिद्या दोऊ।।
verses.transliteration:
go gocara jahaṃ lagi mana jāī. so saba māyā jānehu bhāī.
tehi kara bheda sunahu tumha soū. bidyā apara abidyā doū.
verses.translation: 'Whatever is perceived by the senses, wherever the mind can reach — know all of that to be Maya, O brother. Now hear its two divisions as well: Vidya (knowledge) and Avidya (ignorance).'

verses.original:
एक दुष्ट अतिसय दुखरूपा। जा बस जीव परा भवकूपा।।
एक रचइ जग गुन बस जाकें। प्रभु प्रेरित नहिं निज बल ताकें।।
verses.transliteration:
eka duṣṭa atisaya dukharūpā. jā basa jīva parā bhavakūpā.
eka racai jaga guṇa basa jākeṃ. prabhu prerita nahiṃ nija bala tākeṃ.
verses.translation: 'One (Avidya) is wicked and extremely sorrowful, by which the soul has fallen into the well of worldly existence. The other (Vidya) creates the world, functioning under the Lord's will, having no independent power.'

verses.original:
ग्यान मान जहँ एकउ नाहीं। देख ब्रह्म समान सब माही।।
कहिअ तात सो परम बिरागी। तृन सम सिद्धि तीनि गुन त्यागी।।
verses.transliteration:
gyāna māna jahaṃ ekau nāhīṃ. dekha brahma samāna saba māhī.
kahiahiṃ tāta so parama birāgī. tṛna sama siddhi tīni guṇa tyāgī.
verses.translation: 'Where there is no trace of pride or self-importance, and one sees Brahman equally in all — such a one, dear brother, is called supremely detached, regarding even supernatural powers as insignificant as a blade of grass.'

verses.original:
माया ईस न आपु कहुँ जान कहिअ सो जीव।
बंध मोच्छ प्रद सर्बपर माया प्रेरक सीव।।15।।
verses.transliteration:
māyā īsa na āpu kahuṃ jāna kahiahiṃ so jīva.
bandha mocchaprda sarbapara māyā preraka sīva. 15.
verses.translation: That which is subject to Maya and does not know itself — that is called the individual soul (jiva). That which grants both bondage and liberation, which is supreme over all and which directs Maya — that is Shiva (God). ||15||

verses.original:
धर्म तें बिरति जोग तें ग्याना। ग्यान मोच्छप्रद बेद बखाना।।
जातें बेगि द्रवउँ मैं भाई। सो मम भगति भगत सुखदाई।।
verses.transliteration:
dharma teṃ birati joga teṃ gyānā. gyāna mocchaparada beda bakhānā.
jāteṃ begi dravauṃ maiṃ bhāī. so mama bhagati bhagata sukhadāī.
verses.translation: 'From righteousness comes detachment; from yoga comes knowledge; knowledge grants liberation, say the Vedas. But that by which I am quickly moved, O brother — that is My devotion, the giver of happiness to devotees.'

verses.original:
सो सुतंत्र अवलंब न आना। तेहि आधीन ग्यान बिग्याना।।
भगति तात अनुपम सुखमूला। मिलइ जो संत होइँ अनुकूला।।
verses.transliteration:
so sutantra avalamba na ānā. tehi ādhīna gyāna bigyānā.
bhagati tāta anupama sukhamūlā. milai jo santa hoīṃ anukūlā.
verses.translation: 'That devotion is independent, needing no other support; knowledge and wisdom are dependent on it. Devotion, dear one, is the incomparable root of all happiness; it is obtained when the saints become favorable.'

verses.original:
भगति कि साधन कहउँ बखानी। सुगम पंथ मोहि पावहिं प्रानी।।
प्रथमहिं बिप्र चरन अति प्रीती। निज निज कर्म निरत श्रुति रीती।।
verses.transliteration:
bhagati ki sādhana kahauṃ bakhānī. sugama pantha mohi pāvahiṃ prānī.
prathamahiṃ bipra carana ati prītī. nija nija karma nirata śruti rītī.
verses.translation: 'Shall I describe the means of devotion? — that easy path by which beings attain Me? First, deep love for the feet of the Brahmins; devotion to one's own duties as prescribed by the Vedas.'

verses.original:
एहि कर फल पुनि बिषय बिरागा। तब मम धर्म उपज अनुरागा।।
श्रवनादिक नव भक्ति दृढ़ाहीं। मम लीला रति अति मन माहीं।।
verses.transliteration:
ehi kara phala puni biṣaya birāgā. taba mama dharma upaja anurāgā.
śravaṇādika nava bhakti dṛḍhāhīṃ. mama līlā rati ati mana māhīṃ.
verses.translation: 'The fruit of this is detachment from worldly pleasures; then love for My dharma (way) arises. The nine forms of devotion — beginning with hearing — become firm, and great delight in My divine play fills the mind.'

verses.original:
संत चरन पंकज अति प्रेमा। मन क्रम बचन भजन दृढ़ नेमा।।
गुरु पितु मातु बंधु पति देवा। सब मोहि कहँ जाने दृढ़ सेवा।।
verses.transliteration:
santa carana paṅkaja ati premā. mana krama bacana bhajana dṛḍha nemā.
guru pitu mātu bandhu pati devā. saba mohi kahaṃ jāne dṛḍha sevā.
verses.translation: 'Great love for the lotus feet of saints; firm discipline of worship in thought, word, and deed. Knowing guru, father, mother, kin, spouse, and gods — all as secondary to Me, with steadfast service.'

verses.original:
मम गुन गावत पुलक सरीरा। गदगद गिरा नयन बह नीरा।।
काम आदि मद दंभ न जाकें। तात निरंतर बस मैं ताकें।।
verses.transliteration:
mama guṇa gāvata pulaka sarīrā. gadagada girā nayana baha nīrā.
kāma ādi mada dambha na jākeṃ. tāta nirantara basa maiṃ tākeṃ.
verses.translation: 'Singing My glories with body thrilling, voice choked with emotion, and eyes streaming with tears. Free from lust, pride, hypocrisy, and other vices — I am always in the power of such a devotee.'

verses.original:
बचन कर्म मन मोरि गति भजनु करहिं निःकाम।।
तिन्ह के हृदय कमल महुँ करउँ सदा बिश्राम।।16।।
verses.transliteration:
bacana karma mana morī gati bhajanu karahiṃ niḥkāma.
tinh ke hṛdaya kamala mahuṃ karauṃ sadā biśrāma. 16.
verses.translation: Those who worship Me selflessly in thought, word, and deed, making Me their sole refuge — in the lotus of their hearts, I take My eternal rest. ||16||

verses.original:
भगति जोग सुनि अति सुख पावा। लछिमन प्रभु चरनन्हि सिरु नावा।।
एहि बिधि गए कछुक दिन बीती। कहत बिराग ग्यान गुन नीती।।
verses.transliteration:
bhagati joga suni ati sukha pāvā. lacchimana prabhu carananhi siru nāvā.
ehi bidhi gae kachuka dina bītī. kahata birāga gyāna guṇa nītī.
verses.translation: Hearing the discourse on devotion, Lakshmana felt supreme joy and bowed his head at the Lord's feet. In this manner some days passed, spent in discussions on detachment, knowledge, virtue, and conduct.

verses.original:
सूपनखा रावन कै बहिनी। दुष्ट हृदय दारुन जस अहिनी।।
पंचबटी सो गइ एक बारा। देखि बिकल भइ जुगल कुमारा।।
verses.transliteration:
sūpanakhā rāvana kai bahinī. duṣṭa hṛdaya dāruna jasa ahinī.
pañcabaṭī so gaī eka bārā. dekhi bikala bhaī jugala kumārā.
verses.translation: Surpanakha, Ravana's sister — wicked at heart, terrible like a serpent — once came to Panchavati. Seeing the two handsome princes, she became infatuated.

verses.original:
भ्राता पिता पुत्र उरगारी। पुरुष मनोहर निरखत नारी।।
होइ बिकल सक मनहि न रोकी। जिमि रबिमनि द्रव रबिहि बिलोकी।।
verses.transliteration:
bhrātā pitā putra uragārī. puruṣa manohara nirakhata nārī.
hoi bikala saka manahi na rokī. jimi rabimani drava rabihi bilokī.
verses.translation: Whether it be a brother, father, son, or even Garuda — when a woman sees a handsome man, she becomes agitated. She lost her composure and could not restrain her mind, just as a sun-stone melts upon seeing the sun.

verses.original:
रुचिर रुप धरि प्रभु पहिं जाई। बोली बचन बहुत मुसुकाई।।
तुम्ह सम पुरुष न मो सम नारी। यह सँजोग बिधि रचा बिचारी।।
verses.transliteration:
rucira rūpa dhari prabhu pahiṃ jāī. bolī bacana bahuta musakāī.
tumha sama puruṣa na mo sama nārī. yaha saṃjoga bidhi racā bicārī.
verses.translation: Assuming a beautiful form, she went to the Lord and spoke many smiling words: 'There is no man like you, nor any woman like me; the Creator must have planned this union.'

verses.original:
मम अनुरूप पुरुष जग माहीं। देखेउँ खोजि लोक तिहु नाहीं।।
ताते अब लगि रहिउँ कुमारी। मनु माना कछु तुम्हहि निहारी।।
verses.transliteration:
mama anurūpa puruṣa jaga māhīṃ. dekheuṃ khoji loka tihu nāhīṃ.
tāte aba lagi rahiuṃ kumārī. manu mānā kachu tumhahi nihārī.
verses.translation: 'There is no man in all the three worlds worthy of me — I have searched everywhere. Therefore I have remained unmarried till now; my heart has found some satisfaction in seeing you.'

verses.original:
सीतहि चितइ कही प्रभु बाता। अहइ कुआर मोर लघु भ्राता।।
गइ लछिमन रिपु भगिनी जानी। प्रभु बिलोकि बोले मृदु बानी।।
verses.transliteration:
sītahi citai kahī prabhu bātā. ahai kuāra mora laghu bhrātā.
gaī lacchimana ripu bhaginī jānī. prabhu biloki bole mṛdu bānī.
verses.translation: Looking at Sita, the Lord spoke: 'My younger brother is unmarried.' She went to Lakshmana, who recognized her as the sister of the enemy. Looking at the Lord, he spoke gently:

verses.original:
सुंदरि सुनु मैं उन्ह कर दासा। पराधीन नहिं तोर सुपासा।।
प्रभु समर्थ कोसलपुर राजा। जो कछु करहिं उनहि सब छाजा।।
verses.transliteration:
sundari sunu maiṃ unha kara dāsā. parādhīna nahiṃ tora supāsā.
prabhu samartha kosalapura rājā. jo kachu karahiṃ unahi saba chājā.
verses.translation: 'Fair one, listen — I am His servant, not my own master; your good fortune lies not with me. The Lord is mighty, the King of Kosala; whatever He does befits Him.'

verses.original:
सेवक सुख चह मान भिखारी। ब्यसनी धन सुभ गति बिभिचारी।।
लोभी जसु चह चार गुमानी। नभ दुहि दूध चहत ए प्रानी।।
verses.transliteration:
sevaka sukha caha māna bhikhārī. byasanī dhana subha gati bibhicārī.
lobhī jasu caha cāra gumānī. nabha duhi dūdha cahata e prānī.
verses.translation: 'A servant wanting happiness, a beggar wanting honor, an addict wanting wealth, an adulterer wanting a good afterlife, a miser wanting fame, an arrogant person wanting all four — these people are milking the sky for milk.'

verses.original:
पुनि फिरि राम निकट सो आई। प्रभु लछिमन पहिं बहुरि पठाई।।
लछिमन कहा तोहि सो बरई। जो तृन तोरि लाज परिहरई।।
verses.transliteration:
puni phiri rāma nikaṭa so āī. prabhu lacchimana pahiṃ bahuri paṭhāī.
lacchimana kahā tohi so baraī. jo tṛna tori lāja pariharaī.
verses.translation: She went back again to Rama; the Lord sent her back to Lakshmana once more. Lakshmana said: 'You suit only one who, breaking off a blade of grass, has cast away all shame.'

verses.original:
तब खिसिआनि राम पहिं गई। रूप भयंकर प्रगटत भई।।
सीतहि सभय देखि रघुराई। कहा अनुज सन सयन बुझाई।।
verses.transliteration:
taba khisiānī rāma pahiṃ gaī. rūpa bhayaṅkara pragaṭata bhaī.
sītahi sabhaya dekhi raghurāī. kahā anuja sana sayana bujhāī.
verses.translation: Then, enraged, she went to Rama and revealed her terrifying true form. Seeing Sita frightened, the Lord of the Raghus signaled to His younger brother with a meaningful look.

verses.original:
लछिमन अति लाघवँ सो नाक कान बिनु कीन्हि।
ताके कर रावन कहँ मनौ चुनौती दीन्हि।।17।।
verses.transliteration:
lacchimana ati lāghavaṃ so nāka kāna binu kīnhi.
tāke kara rāvana kahaṃ manau cunautī dīnhi. 17.
verses.translation: With great swiftness, Lakshmana cut off her nose and ears. Through her, it was as if a challenge had been issued to Ravana. ||17||

verses.original:
नाक कान बिनु भइ बिकरारा। जनु स्त्रव सैल गैरु कै धारा।।
खर दूषन पहिं गइ बिलपाता। धिग धिग तव पौरुष बल भ्राता।।
verses.transliteration:
nāka kāna binu bhaī bikarārā. janu strava saila gairu kai dhārā.
khara dūṣana pahiṃ gaī bilapātā. dhiga dhiga tava pauruṣa bala bhrātā.
verses.translation: Without nose and ears, she became hideous — as if a mountain of red ochre were flowing with streams. She went wailing to Khara and Dushana: 'Shame, shame on your manhood and strength, O brothers!'

verses.original:
तेहि पूछा सब कहेसि बुझाई। जातुधान सुनि सेन बनाई।।
धाए निसिचर निकर बरूथा। जनु सपच्छ कज्जल गिरि जूथा।।
verses.transliteration:
tehi pūchā saba kahesi bujhāī. jātudhāna suni senā banāī.
dhāe nisicara nikara barūthā. janu sapaccha kajjala giri jūthā.
verses.translation: He asked, and she told him everything. Hearing it, the demons raised an army. Hordes of demons charged forth like herds of winged mountains of dark soot.

verses.original:
नाना बाहन नानाकारा। नानायुध धर घोर अपारा।।
सुपनखा आगें करि लीनी। असुभ रूप श्रुति नासा हीनी।।
verses.transliteration:
nānā bāhana nānākārā. nānāyudha dhara ghora apārā.
supanakhā āgeṃ kari līnī. asubha rūpa śruti nāsā hīnī.
verses.translation: On various mounts, in various forms, bearing various terrible weapons beyond count. They placed Surpanakha in front — in her inauspicious form, bereft of ears and nose.

verses.original:
असगुन अमित होहिं भयकारी। गनहिं न मृत्यु बिबस सब झारी।।
गर्जहि तर्जहिं गगन उड़ाहीं। देखि कटकु भट अति हरषाहीं।।
verses.transliteration:
asaguna amita hohiṃ bhayakārī. ganahiṃ na mṛtyu bibasa saba jhārī.
garjahi tarjahi gagana uḍāhīṃ. dekhi kaṭaku bhaṭa ati haraṣāhīṃ.
verses.translation: Countless fearful omens appeared, but all of them, driven by death, disregarded them. They roared, threatened, and flew through the sky; seeing the army, the warriors rejoiced greatly.

verses.original:
कोउ कह जिअत धरहु द्वौ भाई। धरि मारहु तिय लेहु छड़ाई।।
धूरि पूरि नभ मंडल रहा। राम बोलाइ अनुज सन कहा।।
verses.transliteration:
kou kaha jiata dharahu dvau bhāī. dhari mārahu tiya lehu chaḍāī.
dhūri pūri nabha maṇḍala rahā. rāma bolāi anuja sana kahā.
verses.translation: Some said: 'Capture both brothers alive; capture them, kill them, and free the woman.' Dust filled the sky. Rama called His brother and said:

verses.original:
लै जानकिहि जाहु गिरि कंदर। आवा निसिचर कटकु भयंकर।।
रहेहु सजग सुनि प्रभु कै बानी। चले सहित श्री सर धनु पानी।।
verses.transliteration:
lai jānakihi jāhu giri kandara. āvā nisicara kaṭaku bhayankara.
rahehu sajaga suni prabhu kai bānī. cale sahita śrī sara dhanu pānī.
verses.translation: 'Take Janaki and go to a mountain cave. A fearsome demon army has arrived. Stay alert.' Hearing the Lord's words, Lakshmana departed with Sita, bow and arrows in hand.

verses.original: देखि राम रिपुदल चलि आवा। बिहसि कठिन कोदंड चढ़ावा।।
verses.transliteration: dekhi rāma ripudala cali āvā. bihasi kaṭhina kodaṇḍa caḍhāvā.
verses.translation: Seeing the enemy host approaching, Rama smiled and strung His mighty bow.

verses.original:
प्रभु बिलोकि सर सकहिं न डारी। थकित भई रजनीचर धारी।।
सचिव बोलि बोले खर दूषन। यह कोउ नृपबालक नर भूषन।।
verses.transliteration:
prabhu biloki sara sakahiṃ na ḍārī. thakita bhaī rajanīcara dhārī.
saciva boli bole khara dūṣana. yaha kou nṛpabālaka nara bhūṣana.
verses.translation: Seeing the Lord, the demon army could not even release their arrows; they stood paralyzed. Khara and Dushana called their ministers and said: 'This is some young prince, an ornament among men.'

verses.original:
नाग असुर सुर नर मुनि जेते। देखे जिते हते हम केते।।
हम भरि जन्म सुनहु सब भाई। देखी नहिं असि सुंदरताई।।
verses.transliteration:
nāga asura sura nara muni jete. dekhe jite hate hama kete.
hama bhari janma sunahu saba bhāī. dekhī nahiṃ asi sundaratāī.
verses.translation: 'Of all the serpents, demons, gods, men, and sages — how many have we seen, conquered, and slain! In our whole lives, listen, O brothers, we have never seen such beauty.'

verses.original:
जद्यपि भगिनी कीन्ह कुरूपा। बध लायक नहिं पुरुष अनूपा।।
देहु तुरत निज नारि दुराई। जीअत भवन जाहु द्वौ भाई।।
verses.transliteration:
jadyapi bhaginī kīnha kurūpā. badha lāyaka nahiṃ puruṣa anūpā.
dehu turata nija nāri durāī. jīata bhavana jāhu dvau bhāī.
verses.translation: 'Although our sister has been disfigured, these peerless men do not deserve to be slain. Give up the woman quickly and let the two brothers go home alive.'

verses.original:
मोर कहा तुम्ह ताहि सुनावहु। तासु बचन सुनि आतुर आवहु।।
दूतन्ह कहा राम सन जाई। सुनत राम बोले मुसकाई।।
verses.transliteration:
mora kahā tumha tāhi sunāvahu. tāsu bacana suni ātura āvahu.
dūtanh kahā rāma sana jāī. sunata rāma bole musakāī.
verses.translation: 'Tell them my message and then quickly return with their reply.' The messengers went and spoke to Rama. Hearing them, Rama smiled and said:

verses.original:
हम छत्री मृगया बन करहीं। तुम्ह से खल मृग खौजत फिरहीं।।
रिपु बलवंत देखि नहिं डरहीं। एक बार कालहु सन लरहीं।।
verses.transliteration:
hama chatrī mṛgayā bana karahīṃ. tumha se khala mṛga khaujata phirahīṃ.
ripu balavanta dekhi nahiṃ ḍarahīṃ. ekai bāra kālahu sana larahīṃ.
verses.translation: 'We are Kshatriyas; we hunt in the forest. We roam about seeking wicked prey like you. We do not fear a powerful enemy; we would even fight Death itself.'

verses.original:
जद्यपि मनुज दनुज कुल घालक। मुनि पालक खल सालक बालक।।
जौं न होइ बल घर फिरि जाहू। समर बिमुख मैं हतउँ न काहू।।
verses.transliteration:
jadyapi manuja danuja kula ghālaka. muni pālaka khala sālaka bālaka.
jauṃ na hoi bala ghara phiri jāhū. samara bimukha maiṃ hatauṃ na kāhū.
verses.translation: 'Though we are humans, we are the destroyers of demon clans, protectors of sages, and punishers of the wicked — yet we are young. If you lack strength, go home — I do not slay those who flee from battle.'

verses.original:
रन चढ़ि करिअ कपट चतुराई। रिपु पर कृपा परम कदराई।।
दूतन्ह जाइ तुरत सब कहेऊ। सुनि खर दूषन उर अति दहेऊ।।
verses.transliteration:
rana caḍhi kariahiṃ kapaṭa caturāī. ripu para kṛpā parama kadarāī.
dūtanh jāi turata saba kaheū. suni khara dūṣana ura ati daheū.
verses.translation: 'One who engages in deceit and cunning on the battlefield, or shows mercy to the enemy — that is supreme cowardice.' The messengers went and told everything. Hearing it, Khara and Dushana burned with rage.

verses.original:
तब चले जान बबान कराल। फुंकरत जनु बहु ब्याल।।
कोपेउ समर श्रीराम। चले बिसिख निसित निकाम।।
अवलोकि खरतर तीर। मुरि चले निसिचर बीर।।
भए क्रुद्ध तीनिउ भाइ। जो भागि रन ते जाइ।।
तेहि बधब हम निज पानि। फिरे मरन मन महुँ ठानि।।
आयुध अनेक प्रकार। सनमुख ते करहिं प्रहार।।
रिपु परम कोपे जानि। प्रभु धनुष सर संधानि।।
छाँड़े बिपुल नाराच। लगे कटन बिकट पिसाच।।
उर सीस भुज कर चरन। जहँ तहँ लगे महि परन।।
चिक्करत लागत बान। धर परत कुधर समान।।
भट कटत तन सत खंड। पुनि उठत करि पाषंड।।
नभ उड़त बहु भुज मुंड। बिनु मौलि धावत रुंड।।
खग कंक काक सृगाल। कटकटहिं कठिन कराल।।
कटकटहिं ज़ंबुक भूत प्रेत पिसाच खर्पर संचहीं।
बेताल बीर कपाल ताल बजाइ जोगिनि नंचहीं।।
रघुबीर बान प्रचंड खंडहिं भटन्ह के उर भुज सिरा।
जहँ तहँ परहिं उठि लरहिं धर धरु धरु करहिं भयकर गिरा।।
अंतावरीं गहि उड़त गीध पिसाच कर गहि धावहीं।।
संग्राम पुर बासी मनहुँ बहु बाल गुड़ी उड़ावहीं।।
मारे पछारे उर बिदारे बिपुल भट कहँरत परे।
अवलोकि निज दल बिकल भट तिसिरादि खर दूषन फिरे।।
सर सक्ति तोमर परसु सूल कृपान एकहि बारहीं।
करि कोप श्रीरघुबीर पर अगनित निसाचर डारहीं।।
प्रभु निमिष महुँ रिपु सर निवारि पचारि डारे सायका।
दस दस बिसिख उर माझ मारे सकल निसिचर नायका।।
महि परत उठि भट भिरत मरत न करत माया अति घनी।
सुर डरत चौदह सहस प्रेत बिलोकि एक अवध धनी।।
सुर मुनि सभय प्रभु देखि मायानाथ अति कौतुक कर् यो।
देखहि परसपर राम करि संग्राम रिपुदल लरि मर् यो।।
verses.transliteration:
taba cale jāna babāna karāla. phuṃkarata janu bahu byāla.
kopeu samara śrīrāma. cale bisikha nisita nikāma.
avaloki kharatara tīra. muri cale nisicara bīra.
bhae kruddha tīniu bhāi. jo bhāgi rana te jāi.
tehi badhaba hama nija pāni. phire marana mana mahuṃ ṭhāni.
āyudha aneka prakāra. sanamukha te karahiṃ prahāra.
ripu parama kope jāni. prabhu dhanuṣa sara sandhāni.
chāṃḍe bipula nārāca. lage kaṭana bikaṭa pisāca.
ura sīsa bhuja kara carana. jahaṃ tahaṃ lage mahi parana.
cikkarata lāgata bāna. dhara parata kudhara samāna.
bhaṭa kaṭata tana sata khaṇḍa. puni uṭhata kari pāṣaṇḍa.
nabha uḍata bahu bhuja muṇḍa. binu mauli dhāvata ruṇḍa.
khaga kaṅka kāka sṛgāla. kaṭakaṭahiṃ kaṭhina karāla.
kaṭakaṭahiṃ jambuka bhūta preta pisāca kharpara sañcahīṃ.
betāla bīra kapāla tāla bajāi joginī nañcahīṃ.
raghubīra bāna pracaṇḍa khaṇḍahiṃ bhaṭanh ke ura bhuja sirā.
jahaṃ tahaṃ parahiṃ uṭhi larahiṃ dhara dharu dharu karahiṃ bhayakara girā.
antāvarīṃ gahi uḍata gīdha pisāca kara gahi dhāvahīṃ.
saṃgrāma pura bāsī manahuṃ bahu bāla guḍī uḍāvahīṃ.
māre pachāre ura bidāre bipula bhaṭa kahaṃrata pare.
avaloki nija dala bikala bhaṭa tisirādi khara dūṣana phire.
sara sakti tomara parasu sūla kṛpāna ekahi bārahīṃ.
kari kopa śrīraghubīra para aganita nisācara ḍārahīṃ.
prabhu nimiṣa mahuṃ ripu sara nivāri pacāri ḍāre sāyakā.
dasa dasa bisikha ura mājha māre sakala nisicara nāyakā.
mahi parata uṭhi bhaṭa bhirata marata na karata māyā ati ghanī.
sura ḍarata caudaha sahasa preta biloki eka avadha dhanī.
sura muni sabhaya prabhu dekhi māyānātha ati kautuka karyo.
dekhahi parasapara rāma kari saṃgrāma ripudala lari maryo.
verses.translation: Then the terrible warriors charged, hissing like countless serpents. Sri Rama became wrathful in battle, and sharp, relentless arrows flew forth. Seeing the keen arrows, the demon warriors turned and fled. The three demon brothers grew furious: 'Whoever flees from battle, we shall slay him ourselves!' They turned back, having resolved to die. With weapons of many kinds, they attacked head-on. Knowing the enemy supremely enraged, the Lord fitted arrows to His bow and released countless iron-shafted arrows that began to cut down the terrible demons. Heads, arms, hands, and feet began falling everywhere on the ground. Struck by arrows, warriors fell like mountains. Demons, cut into a hundred pieces, would rise again through their sorcery. In the sky flew many arms and heads; headless trunks ran about. Vultures, crows, and jackals gnashed their terrible teeth. Jackals, ghosts, evil spirits, and goblins gathered skulls; mighty vampires clapped skull-cymbals while yoginis danced. Raghubir's fierce arrows shattered the warriors' chests, arms, and heads. Body parts fell here and there, rose and fought again, crying 'Seize them! Seize them!' in terrifying voices. Vultures flew holding entrails; ghouls ran grasping hands. It was as if the residents of the battlefield-city were flying many kites. Slain, felled, chests torn open — countless warriors lay screaming. Seeing their army in distress, Trishira, Khara, and Dushana returned. Arrows, spears, javelins, axes, tridents, swords — all at once, in fury, the countless demons hurled them at Sri Raghubir. The Lord in an instant parried the enemy's arrows and, challenging them, released His own shafts. He struck each demon chief with ten arrows in the chest. Warriors falling, rising, fighting again, dying but not dying — creating dense illusions. The gods feared, beholding fourteen thousand demons against the lone Lord of Ayodhya. Seeing the gods and sages frightened, the Lord of Maya performed a great marvel — each demon saw Rama fighting alongside him, and the enemy army fought and destroyed itself.

verses.original:
जब रघुनाथ समर रिपु जीते। सुर नर मुनि सब के भय बीते।।
तब लछिमन सीतहि लै आए। प्रभु पद परत हरषि उर लाए।
verses.transliteration:
jaba raghunātha samara ripu jīte. sura nara muni saba ke bhaya bīte.
taba lacchimana sītahi lai āe. prabhu pada parata haraṣi ura lāe.
verses.translation: When the Lord of the Raghus conquered the enemy in battle, the fears of gods, men, and sages were removed. Then Lakshmana brought Sita back; she fell at the Lord's feet, and He joyfully embraced them.

verses.original:
सीता चितव स्याम मृदु गाता। परम प्रेम लोचन न अघाता।।
पंचवटीं बसि श्रीरघुनायक। करत चरित सुर मुनि सुखदायक।।
verses.transliteration:
sītā citava syāma mṛdu gātā. parama prema locana na aghātā.
pañcavaṭīṃ basi śrīraghunāyaka. karata carita sura muni sukhadāyaka.
verses.translation: Sita gazed at the Lord's dark, tender form; her eyes could not get enough of Him, such was her supreme love. Dwelling at Panchavati, the Lord of the Raghus performed deeds that delighted gods and sages.

verses.original:
धुआँ देखि खरदूषन केरा। जाइ सुपनखाँ रावन प्रेरा।।
बोलि बचन क्रोध करि भारी। देस कोस कै सुरति बिसारी।।
verses.transliteration:
dhuāṃ dekhi kharadūṣana kerā. jāi supanakhāṃ rāvana prerā.
boli bacana krodha kari bhārī. desa kosa kai surati bisārī.
verses.translation: Seeing the destruction of Khara and Dushana, Surpanakha went and incited Ravana. She spoke words of great anger: 'You have forgotten the welfare of your kingdom and realm!'

verses.original:
करसि पान सोवसि दिनु राती। सुधि नहिं तव सिर पर आराती।।
राज नीति बिनु धन बिनु धर्मा। हरिहि समर्पे बिनु सतकर्मा।।
verses.transliteration:
karasi pāna sovasi dinu rātī. sudhi nahiṃ tava sira para ārātī.
rāja nīti binu dhana binu dharmā. harihi samarpe binu satakarmā.
verses.translation: 'You drink and sleep day and night, with no awareness that enemies threaten your very head. Statecraft without wealth, dharma without surrendering to Hari, good deeds without true purpose —'

verses.original:
बिद्या बिनु बिबेक उपजाएँ। श्रम फल पढ़े किएँ अरु पाएँ।।
संग ते जती कुमंत्र ते राजा। मान ते ग्यान पान तें लाजा।।
verses.transliteration:
bidyā binu bibeka upajāeṃ. śrama phala paḍhe kieṃ aru pāeṃ.
saṅga te jatī kumantra te rājā. māna te gyāna pāna teṃ lājā.
verses.translation: 'Learning without wisdom, reading and acquiring knowledge to no purpose; an ascetic ruined by attachment, a king ruined by bad counsel, wisdom destroyed by pride, modesty destroyed by drink —'

verses.original: प्रीति प्रनय बिनु मद ते गुनी। नासहि बेगि नीति अस सुनी।।
verses.transliteration: prīti pranaya binu mada te gunī. nāsahi begi nīti asa sunī.
verses.translation: 'Love without affection, pride destroying a learned man — all these are quickly destroyed; such is the teaching I have heard.'

verses.original:
रिपु रुज पावक पाप प्रभु अहि गनिअ न छोट करि।
अस कहि बिबिध बिलाप करि लागी रोदन करन।।21(क)।।
सभा माझ परि ब्याकुल बहु प्रकार कह रोइ।
तोहि जिअत दसकंधर मोरि कि असि गति होइ।।21(ख)।।
verses.transliteration:
ripu ruja pāvaka pāpa prabhu ahi ganiahiṃ na choṭa kari.
asa kahi bibidha bilāpa kari lāgī rodana karana. 21(ka).
sabhā mājha parī byākula bahu prakāra kaha roi.
tohi jiata dasakandhara morī ki asi gati hoi. 21(kha).
verses.translation:
Foes, disease, fire, sin, a master, serpents — never regard these as small. So saying, she lamented in many ways and began to weep. ||21a||
Falling distraught in the middle of the court, she cried in many ways: 'Can this be my fate while you, O ten-headed one, are alive?' ||21b||

verses.original:
सुनत सभासद उठे अकुलाई। समुझाई गहि बाहँ उठाई।।
कह लंकेस कहसि निज बाता। केंइँ तव नासा कान निपाता।।
verses.transliteration:
sunata sabhāsada uṭhe akulāī. samujhāī gahi bāhaṃ uṭhāī.
kaha laṅkesa kahasi nija bātā. keīṃ tava nāsā kāna nipātā.
verses.translation: Hearing this, the courtiers rose in alarm; they consoled her, took her by the arm, and raised her. The Lord of Lanka asked: 'Tell me what happened. Who cut off your nose and ears?'

verses.original:
अवध नृपति दसरथ के जाए। पुरुष सिंघ बन खेलन आए।।
समुझि परी मोहि उन्ह कै करनी। रहित निसाचर करिहहिं धरनी।।
verses.transliteration:
avadha nṛpati dasaratha ke jāe. puruṣa siṃgha bana khelana āe.
samujhi parī mohi unha kai karanī. rahita nisācara karihahiṃ dharanī.
verses.translation: 'They are the sons of King Dasharatha of Ayodhya — lion-like men who have come to sport in the forest. Understanding their deeds, I now realize they will rid the earth of all demons.'

verses.original:
जिन्ह कर भुजबल पाइ दसानन। अभय भए बिचरत मुनि कानन।।
देखत बालक काल समाना। परम धीर धन्वी गुन नाना।।
verses.transliteration:
jinha kara bhujabala pāi dasānana. abhaya bhae bicarata muni kānana.
dekhata bālaka kāla samānā. parama dhīra dhanvī guṇa nānā.
verses.translation: 'Through the might of whose arms, O ten-headed one, the sages have become fearless and roam the forests freely. In appearance mere boys, they are Death incarnate — supremely resolute, skilled archers with many virtues.'

verses.original:
अतुलित बल प्रताप द्वौ भ्राता। खल बध रत सुर मुनि सुखदाता।।
सोभाधाम राम अस नामा। तिन्ह के संग नारि एक स्यामा।।
